questions.question: What is the primary function of the Erosion operation in Morphological Image Processing?
options: C) To remove noise and details
questions.explanation: The Erosion operation removes small details and noise from an image by removing the smallest connected components.

questions.question: Which class of morphological operations is used to separate objects from their surroundings?
questions.explanation: The Opening operation is used to separate objects from their surroundings by removing small details and noise.

questions.question: What is the effect of the Dilation operation on an image?
options: A) To remove noise and details
C) To fill holes and expand edges
questions.explanation: The Dilation operation fills holes and expands edges in an image.

questions.question: Which morphological operation is the inverse of the Erosion operation?
questions.explanation: The Dilation operation is the inverse of the Erosion operation.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Opening operation in Morphological Image Processing?
options: B) To remove noise and details
questions.explanation: The Opening operation is used to separate objects from their surroundings by removing small details and noise.
